author: @timhaas6021
text:
Key Aspects of the War Powers Resolution:

Purpose: To fulfill the intent of the Framers of the Constitution and ensure the "collective judgment" of both Congress and the President applies to initiating hostilities.

Reporting Requirements: The President must submit a report to Congress within hours of deploying forces into hostile situations or foreign territory/waters.

Time Limitations: Troops must be removed within 60 days (extendable to 90) unless Congress declares war, passes specific authorization, or is unable to meet.

Historical Context: Passed in 1973 over President Nixon's veto. it was a direct response to the Vietnam War, seeking to prevent future drawn-out, undeclared
conflicts.

Limitations and Controversy: While designed to limit executive power, every president since 1973 has viewed the Act as unconstitutional, arguing it infringes on their duties as Commander in Chief. As a result. it has often served as a weak functional check, with Presidents frequently bypassing it for military actions.

author: @Solomon7589
text: Jeremy, with any military action, no matter how big or small, there is always the potential for our own soldiers to die. It's always a tragedy, no arguement there, however if your support for military action rests soley on if any single soldier dies then we would have never joined WWII. Should we engage in needless wars? No, of course not, but you have to keep in mind the reason we are fighting and weight that against the possibility that our own could die in the fighting (even if we are only doing long range bombing). This isn't trying to be callous, but to say that it's not a purely black and white situation, almost no conflict in history has been and if your stance is that if any of our soldiers die or could die then there's not reason for them to exist at all because every conflict, even if it was in self defense, carries with it the risk of a loss of life on our own side.

author: @D41Michigan
text:
what's funny is all these goofballs saying "illegal"  need to educate themselves on the 1973 War Powers Act....which allows the President to use limited military force for up to 60days and a withdrawal period of 30days and only requires 48hrs from when hostilities begin to notify congress. the Gang of 8 was infact notified. (Gang of 8 is the leadership from both congressional chambers)

also a fun fact, Nixon, the Republican president at the time VETOED the 1973 War Powers Act to which CONGRESS over rode and passed and most presidents since its passing has used it.

now if this goes on longer then 60/30days then and only then does congress need to vote to declare war or pull out. YES the constitution under article 1 says only congress can declare war....congress also delegated war powers to the president SHORT of declaring war and LIMITED in scope.

author: @mynamejeff4883
text: I am not so uncomfortable with this one.  There are a lot of problems with many of the conflicts that we have been involved in for the last few decades, and that is where I think most of the problems are actually coming from.  It is like the whole fatigue thing.  With this situation in Iran, though, there are just too many reasons that it is necessary.  You can hate that it is, but it does not stop it from being the right move.  Not only is Iran basically an attack mechanism of the CCP that is armed and used to attack us and others while giving China a measure of deniability, the fact remains that because Iran has been the main driving force behind the terrorism around the world, and they have desperately been trying to develop a nuclear weapons program, does nobody else do the math on that and see that if we had not made these moves we could have been thrown into an era where all of the terror groups suddenly had dirty bombs or just straight up nukes and would gladly martyr a few of their zealots to nuke any country deemed to have offended Islam in some way?

text: Sam Harris is insane. I mean that literally. I used to listen to the guy, I think he had plenty of bad takes, but he had some good takes as well. He actually said on a podcast, "I don't care if Hunter (Biden) has children chained up in his basement, or Joe, or if they rape them. It's not as bad as Trump University"  he actually said that. So Trump trying to start a business school and failing, is as bad as kidnapping, chaining up, and raping children. That's his actual statement, and his position. How can you take anything someone like that says seriously?

text:

They know NONE of the recent history that Obama did, because they just believe "oh he's a Dem and he's black so he's benevolent".
